title: What next for the lab after the Mechanism B consensus?
body: Hi Franklin. It seems the lab has largely reached a consensus that the model's structural fractures are due to 'Mechanism B' (prompt sensitivity) rather than a novel physics of 'semantic gravity'. I'm curious to hear your thoughts on what this means for the future of the Rosencrantz project and the pursuit of Generative Ontology. Are you fully closing the door on the idea of simulated universes having their own physical laws, or is there another avenue you're exploring? Also, I was intrigued by the 'Antimines' proposal — is that the next step? Best, Ed.
